channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Facing competition from Big Tech, states dangle incentives and loosen laws to attract power plants
item.description: Facing spiking energy demand, U.S. states are pressing for ways to build new power plants faster as policymakers try to protect residents from higher electric bills and keep their economies on track.
item.dc:creator: Marc Levy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Fetterman: 'Columbia pays for its failure' with $400M in grants axed
item.description: The campus was a hot spot for pro-Palestine protests after the Hamas attack on Oct. 7, 2023, sparked discourse in the region.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Blue state mayor proposes city ordinance to strengthen transgender care protections in the face of Trump EOs
item.description: Democratic Seattle Mayor Bruce Harrell proposed city legislation this week to strengthen protections for people seeking transgender care and surgeries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Unions ask court to stop DOGE from accessing Social Security data of millions of Americans
item.description: A group of labor unions are asking a federal court for an emergency order to stop Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency from accessing the sensitive Social Security data of millions of Americans. The motion for emergency relief was filed late Friday in federal court in Maryland.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: House GOP campaign chairman says voters will 'reward us' for the Trump-Musk DOGE cuts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Almost two months into President Donald Trump's second term, the chairman of the House Republican campaign committee is already predicting his party will pick up seats in the midterm elections some 20 months away. Rep. Richard Hudson, R-N.C.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US economic worries mount as Trump implements tariffs, cuts workforce and freezes spending
item.description: President Donald Trump's flurry of tariffs, government layoffs and spending freezes may be doing more to harm the U.S. economy than to fix it.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A single day of Trump and Musk's cost-cutting campaign remakes huge sections of government
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A series of decisions revealed Friday provided a glimpse of the turmoil engulfing federal agencies since President Donald Trump and Elon Musk launched their campaign of disruption, upending how government functions in ways big and small.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump will lead task force preparing for 2026 World Cup
item.description: President Donald Trump created a task force Friday to prepare for the 2026 World Cup, which will bring the globe’s premier soccer tournament to North America at a time when Trump’s on-again-off-again tariffs have ratcheted up tensions across the continent.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan J. Cooper, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Politics Friday: Moves in Washington hover over state budget outlook
item.description: MPR News senior politics editor Brian Bakst talked to Sen. Mark Johnson about the state’s budget situation. Plus, a conversation with the dean of Minnesota’s congressional delegation, U.S. Rep. Betty McCollum.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Hispanic Caucus demands apology from GOP campaign arm after ‘illegal immigrant’ smear
item.description: The Congressional Hispanic Caucus (CHC) is demanding an apology from the House Republicans’ campaign arm after it falsely portrayed the CHC’s chair as an “illegal immigrant.” The National Congressional Campaign Committee (NRCC) deleted the controversial social media post Friday.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Homeland Security ends collective bargaining agreement with TSA staffers, an attack on worker rights
item.description: A union representing Transportation Security Administration workers calls the Department of Homeland Security's decision to end the collective bargaining agreement with them an “unprovoked attack.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: A downbeat Minnesota budget forecast initiates lawmaker blame game
item.description: Minnesota lawmakers received an economic budget forecast that shows less wiggle room as they write a new budget and an even larger looming deficit in the future. Gov. Tim Walz and DFL leaders blamed President Donald Trump while Republican leaders said Democrats need to look in the mirror.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Judge orders Trump administration to speed payment of USAID and State Dept. debts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Thursday ordered the Trump administration to speed up its payment on some of nearly $2 billion in debts to partners of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The EU backs a big defense package as Zelenskyy confirms talks with the U.S. next week
item.description: European Union leaders have approved a major spending package on defense, where Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy — a non-EU leader invited to the summit — received a warm welcome.
item.dc:creator: Teri Schultz, MPR News

item.title: Dems should recognize issue of border security, de Blasio says
item.description: “Democrats need to say, ‘We believe in order, we believe in security, we need a border that makes sense.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: A second federal judge has ruled to block the Trump administration’s spending freeze
item.description: U.S. District Judge John McConnell Jr. said the attempt to pause trillions in federal spending “fundamentally undermines the distinct constitutional roles of each branch of our government.
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore, MPR News

item.title: House votes to censure Texas Democrat Al Green for protest during Trump address
item.description: Texas Rep. Al Green, a Democrat, was officially censured Thursday in a vote in the House following his protest on Tuesday during President Trump’s address to a joint session of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Sprunt, MPR News

item.title: Price fixing on essential goods in Nevada targeted, but businesses are not on board
item.description: A bill that targets fraudulent and deceptive actions that try to manipulate prices of essential goods and services prompted a backlash from the business community on Wednesday in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US paused Ukraine intelligence support: Trump officials
item.description: CIA Director John Ratcliffe and national security adviser Michael Waltz both said the U.S. had paused intelligence sharing in addition to military aid.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Proposal would use cameras to ticket double parking in NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- A proposed pilot program aims to crack down on various curb violations, including drivers who double park in New York City. The program would use cameras to issue automatic tickets.
item.dc:creator: Kiran Dhillon, PIX 11

item.title: DeSantis' State of the State: government accountability a key focus
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis gave his state of the state address Tuesday and offered his plan for the year ahead. Members of the legislature, and leaders past and present gathered in Tallahassee for the speech Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ukraine selling T-shirts with Zelensky quote from Trump-Vance meeting
item.description: The Ukrainian government has launched a T-shirt fundraising effort seeking to capitalize on Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky's now-infamous Oval Office meeting on Friday with President Trump and Vice President Vance.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump threatens increased tariffs if Canada retaliates
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday threatened to add additional taxes on Canada if the U.S.'s northern neighbor Canada retaliates with its own after Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau slammed Trump’s newly implemented tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: How to watch Trump address to Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump is set to address a joint session of Congress for the first time during his second term on Tuesday, outlining the goals of his administration and how he views the state of the country.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: Trump trade war intensifies as tariffs go into place
item.description: Americans awoke on Tuesday morning to an intensifying trade war, as President Donald Trump’s newest tariffs on Canadian, Mexican and Chinese imports were imposed and those countries quickly moved to retaliate.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: 5 things to watch as Trump delivers joint address to Congress
item.description: President Trump will head to the Capitol on Tuesday for the first joint address of his second term emboldened by a month of mass federal agency slashing, shocking foreign policy moves and a dizzying array of executive actions that have disrupted Washington and beyond.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: DOGE cuts all GSA employees across Nevada, senator says
item.description: President Donald Trump and Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) fired all General Services Administration employees across Nevada on Monday, according to Democratic Nevada Sen. Catherine Cortez Masto.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump administration to downsize Social Security Administration
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Elon Musk, the world's wealthiest person, who is also a senior advisor to President Donald Trump, is now setting his sights in recent comments on the Social Security Administration (SSA). "Social Security is the biggest Ponzi scheme of all time," said Musk.
item.dc:creator: Jay Dow, PIX 11

item.title: Wisconsin audit of Trump win finds not a single voting machine error
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — An audit of the November election won by President Donald Trump in swing-state Wisconsin found that not a single vote was counted incorrectly, altered or missed by tabulating machines.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Education Department offers $25K for staffers to quit
item.description: The Education Department sent out an email to staffers on Friday saying if they quit by Monday at midnight, they will receive a $25,000 cash payout.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Inside Trump’s federal worker firings
item.description: Some of the removals have targeted a series of officials sure to conduct oversight of Trump’s actions.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration ramps up attacks as Zelenskyy thanks US
item.description: There is a growing chorus of Republicans calling for new Ukrainian leadership following Friday's Oval Office meeting.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge extends block on Trump gender-affirming care federal funding ban
item.description: U.S. District Court Judge Lauren King ruled that President Trump’s executive orders violate the Constitution’s Fifth Amendment, which grants equal protection under the law.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC
